heading: Joint task forces
label: 348 Joint task forces

Annual Briefing, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–103, § 2(b)138 Stat. 1584Oct. 1, 2024“(1).—In generalNot later than 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [], and annually thereafter, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall brief—Oct. 1, 2024“(A)the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate;“(B)the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate;“(C)the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives; and“(D)the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.“(2).—TopicsEach briefing required under paragraph (1) shall cover the latest staffing and resource assessment at Joint Task Force-East, including—“(A)(i)a determination of whether the current staffing levels of Joint Task Force-East are sufficient to successfully advance the mission, strategic goals, and objectives of such Joint Task Force; and“(ii)if such determination reveals insufficient staffing levels, the cost, timeline, and strategy for increasing such staffing levels; and“(B)(i)a determination of whether sufficient resources are being provided for Joint Task Force-East in accordance with section 708(b)(7)(a) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (); and6 U.S.C. 348(b)(7)(a)“(ii)if such determination reveals insufficient resource levels, the cost, timeline, and strategy for providing any remaining resource requirements.”

Transition Provisions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–328, div. A, title XIX, § 1901(c)130 Stat. 2670Dec. 23, 2016“An individual serving as a Director of a Joint Task Force of the Department of Homeland Security in existence on the day before the date of the enactment of this section [] may serve as the Director of such Joint Task Force on and after such date of enactment until a Director of such Joint Task Force is appointed pursuant to subparagraph (A) of section 708(b)(3) [], as added by subsection (a) of this section.”Dec. 23, 20166 U.S.C. 348(b)(3)

children.text: threats and trends concerning illicit trafficking and unlawful crossings;
the ability to forecast future shifts in such threats and trends;
the ability to evaluate such threats and trends at a level sufficient to create actionable plans; and
the operational capability to conduct continuous and integrated surveillance of the air, land, and maritime borders of the United States.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary may establish and operate departmental Joint Task Forces to conduct joint operations using personnel and capabilities of the Department for the purposes specified in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: Securing the land and maritime borders of the United States.

children.label: ii
children.text: Homeland security crises.

children.label: iii
children.text: Establishing regionally-based operations.

children.label: I
children.text: do not include operational functions related to incident management, including coordination of operations; and

children.label: II
children.text: are consistent with the requirements of paragraphs (3) and (4)(A) of section 313(c) and , and section 302 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ().section 319(c) of this title42 U.S.C. 5143

children.heading: Responsibilities and functions not reduced
children.label: ii Responsibilities and functions not reduced
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to reduce the responsibilities or functions of the Federal Emergency Management Agency or the Administrator of the Agency under subchapter V of this chapter or any other provision of law, including the diversion of any asset, function, or mission from the Agency or the Administrator of the Agency pursuant to .section 316 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a current senior official of the Department with not less than one year of significant leadership experience at the Department; or

children.label: I
children.text: not less than one year of significant leadership experience in a Federal agency since the establishment of the Department; and

children.label: II
children.text: a demonstrated ability in, knowledge of, and significant experience working on the issues to be addressed by any such Joint Task Force.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: B Extension
children.text: The Secretary may extend the appointment of a Director of a Joint Task Force under subparagraph (A) for not more than two years if the Secretary determines that such an extension is in the best interest of the Department.

children.heading: Joint Task Force deputy directors
children.label: 4 Joint Task Force deputy directors
children.text: For each Joint Task Force, the Secretary shall appoint a Deputy Director who shall be an official of a different component or office of the Department than the Director of such Joint Task Force.

children.label: A
children.text: when established for the purpose referred to in paragraph (2)(A)(i), maintain situational awareness within the areas of responsibility of the Joint Task Force, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: provide operational plans and requirements for standard operating procedures and contingency operations within the areas of responsibility of the Joint Task Force, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: C
children.text: plan and execute joint task force activities within the areas of responsibility of the Joint Task Force, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: set and accomplish strategic objectives through integrated operational planning and execution;

children.label: E
children.text: exercise operational direction over personnel and equipment from components and offices of the Department allocated to the Joint Task Force to accomplish the objectives of the Joint Task Force;

children.label: F
children.text: when established for the purpose referred to in paragraph (2)(A)(i), establish operational and investigative priorities within the areas of responsibility of the Joint Task Force, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: G
children.text: coordinate with foreign governments and other Federal, State, and local agencies, as appropriate, to carry out the mission of the Joint Task Force; and

children.label: H
children.text: carry out other duties and powers the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may, upon request of the Director of a Joint Task Force, and giving appropriate consideration of risk to the other primary missions of the Department, allocate to such Joint Task Force on a temporary basis personnel and equipment of components and offices of the Department.

children.heading: Cost neutrality
children.label: B Cost neutrality
children.text: A Joint Task Force may not require more resources than would have otherwise been required by the Department to carry out the duties assigned to such Joint Task Force if such Joint Task Force had not been established.

children.heading: Location of operations
children.label: C Location of operations
children.text: In establishing a location of operations for a Joint Task Force, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, use existing facilities that integrate efforts of components of the Department and State, local, tribal, or territorial law enforcement or military entities.

children.heading: Consideration of impact
children.label: D Consideration of impact
children.text: When reviewing requests for allocation of component personnel and equipment under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall consider the impact of such allocation on the ability of the donating component or office to carry out the primary missions of the Department, and in the case of the Coast Guard, the missions specified in .section 468 of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: E Limitation
children.text: Personnel and equipment of the Coast Guard allocated under this paragraph may be used only to carry out operations and investigations related to the missions specified in .section 468 of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: F Report
children.text: The Secretary shall, at the time the budget of the President is submitted to Congress for a fiscal year under , submit to the Committee on Homeland Security and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a report on the total funding, personnel, and other resources that each component or office of the Department allocated under this paragraph to each Joint Task Force to carry out the mission of such Joint Task Force during the fiscal year immediately preceding each such report, and a description of the degree to which the resources drawn from each component or office impact the primary mission of such component or office.section 1105(a) of title 31

children.label: A
children.text: each Director of a Joint Task Force shall be provided sufficient resources from relevant components and offices of the Department and the authority necessary to carry out the missions and responsibilities of such Joint Task Force required under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: the resources referred to in subparagraph (A) shall be under the operational authority, direction, and control of the Director of the Joint Task Force to which such resources are assigned; and

children.label: C
children.text: the personnel and equipment of each Joint Task Force shall remain under the administrative direction of the head of the component or office of the Department that provided such personnel or equipment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Each Joint Task Force shall have a staff, composed of personnel from relevant components and offices of the Department, to assist the Director of such Joint Task Force in carrying out the mission and responsibilities of such Joint Task Force.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of personnel of each component or office permanently assigned to each Joint Task Force; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of personnel of each component or office assigned on a temporary basis to each Joint Task Force.

children.label: i
children.text: the mission, strategic goals, and objectives of each Joint Task Force;

children.label: ii
children.text: the criteria for terminating each Joint Task Force;

children.label: I
children.text: targets for each Joint Task Force to achieve by not later than one and three years after such establishment; and

children.label: II
children.text: a description of the methodology used to establish such metrics; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a staffing plan for each Joint Task Force;

children.label: B
children.text: not later than 120 days after , and 120 days after the establishment of a new Joint Task Force, as appropriate, submit to the Committee on Homeland Security and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate the mission, strategic goals, objectives, and metrics established under subparagraph (A); andDecember 23, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: the progress in implementing the outcome-based and other appropriate performance metrics established pursuant to subparagraph (A)(iii);

children.label: ii
children.text: the staffing plan developed for each Joint Task Force pursuant to subparagraph (A)(iv); and

children.label: iii
children.text: any modification to the mission, strategic goals, and objectives of each Joint Task Force, and a description of, and rationale for, any such modifications.

children.label: I
children.text: enhancing coordination within the Department; and

children.label: II
children.text: promoting workforce professional development; and

children.label: ii
children.text: tailor such joint duty training program to improve joint operations as part of the Joint Task Forces.

children.label: i
children.text: National security strategy.

children.label: ii
children.text: Strategic and contingency planning.

children.label: iii
children.text: Command and control of operations under joint command.

children.label: iv
children.text: International engagement.

children.label: v
children.text: The homeland security enterprise.

children.label: vi
children.text: Interagency collaboration.

children.label: vii
children.text: Leadership.

children.label: viii
children.text: Specific subject matters relevant to the Joint Task Force, including matters relating to the missions specified in , to which the joint duty training program is assigned.section 468 of this title

children.heading: Directors and deputy directors
children.label: i Directors and deputy directors
children.text: Except as provided in clauses (iii) and (iv), an individual shall complete the joint duty training program before being appointed Director or Deputy Director of a Joint Task Force.

children.heading: Joint Task Force staff
children.label: ii Joint Task Force staff
children.text: Each official serving on the staff of a Joint Task Force shall complete the joint duty training program within the first year of assignment to such Joint Task Force.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: iii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to the first Director or Deputy Director appointed to a Joint Task Force on or after .December 23, 2016

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: iv Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive the application of clause (i) if the Secretary determines that such a waiver is in the interest of homeland security or necessary to carry out the mission for which a Joint Task Force was established.

children.heading: Notification of Joint Task Force formation or termination
children.label: 11 Notification of Joint Task Force formation or termination
i
children.text: The criteria and conditions required to establish or terminate the Joint Task Force at issue.

children.label: ii
children.text: The primary mission, strategic goals, objectives, and plan of operations of such Joint Task Force.

children.label: iii
children.text: If such notification is a notification of termination, information on the effectiveness of such Joint Task Force as measured by the outcome-based performance metrics and other appropriate performance metrics established pursuant to paragraph (9)(A)(iii).

children.label: iv
children.text: The funding and resources required to establish or terminate such Joint Task Force.

children.label: v
children.text: The number of personnel of each component or office permanently assigned to such Joint Task Force.

children.label: vi
children.text: The number of personnel of each component and office assigned on a temporary basis to such Joint Task Force.

children.label: vii
children.text: If such notification is a notification of establishment, the anticipated costs of establishing and operating such Joint Task Force.

children.label: I
children.text: operations, notwithstanding such termination; and

children.label: II
children.text: activities associated with such termination.

children.label: ix
children.text: The anticipated establishment or actual termination date of such Joint Task Force, as the case may be.

children.heading: Waiver authority
children.label: B Waiver authority
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirement under subparagraph (A) in the event of an emergency circumstance that imminently threatens the protection of human life or property.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than one year after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Homeland Security and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate an assessment of the effectiveness of the Secretary’s utilization of the authority provided under this section for the purposes specified in subsection (b)(2) as among the range of options available to the Secretary to conduct joint operations among departmental components and offices and a review of the Joint Task Forces established under this subsection.December 23, 2022

children.label: i
children.text: an assessment of methodology utilized to determine whether to establish or terminate each Joint Task Force; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of the effectiveness of oversight over each Joint Task Force, with specificity regarding the Secretary’s utilization of outcome-based or other appropriate performance metrics (established pursuant to paragraph (9)(A)(iii)) to evaluate the effectiveness of each Joint Task Force in measuring progress with respect to the mission, strategic goals, and objectives (established pursuant to paragraph (9)(A)(i)) of such Joint Task Force.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: 13 Sunset
children.text: This section expires on .September 30, 2026

children.heading: Joint duty assignment program
children.label: c Joint duty assignment program
children.text: After establishing the joint duty training program under subsection (b)(10), the Secretary shall establish a joint duty assignment program within the Department for the purposes of enhancing coordination in the Department and promoting workforce professional development.
